title: Network Client Connection & Sync

activity_phases.label: TCP Handshake & ConnectPacket Send
steps: Build Connect packet with addressTCP from connection.getRemoteAddressTCP()
→ reset(): clears removed set, Groups, chat, sets connecting=true, quiet=false, clientLoaded=false
→ build ConnectPacket: name, locale, mods, mobile, versionType, color, usid, uuid

activity_phases.label: World Data Receipt
steps: Server streams WorldStream packet (compressed, via ArcConnection.sendStream)
→ InputStreamSender sends StreamBegin(total, type) then StreamChunk[] via TCP

activity_phases.label: Packet Serialization (PacketSerializer)
steps: if compression==1: LZ4 decompress then packet.read(reads)
WRITE path: if ByteBuffer → raw put; if FrameworkMessage → writeFramework()
else: Packet → write id byte, serialize to temp buffer
write length (short); if length<36 or StreamChunk: write uncompressed (flag=0)
else: LZ4 compress into output buffer (flag=1)

activity_phases.label: Ongoing In-Game Sync (update loop)
steps: [if connecting] → timeoutTime += delta; if >dataTimeout(1800f): disconnect with '@disconnect.data'

[every 66ms] Call.clientSnapshot(lastSent++, unitId, dead, x, y, aimX, aimY, rotation, baseRotation, velX, velY, mineTile, boosting, shooting, chatShown, isBuilding, selectedBlock, selectedRotation, plans, camX, camY, camW, camH)
[every 0.5s] collect build plans via control.input.getSyncedPlans(plansOut), chunk into max 75-plan batches, Call.clientPlanSnapshot(id, plans)

activity_phases.label: Receiving Server Snapshots
steps: @Remote entitySnapshot(short amount, byte[] data) [UDP, low priority]:
if entity==null: EntityMapping.map(typeID).get() creates new entity
@Remote hiddenSnapshot(IntSeq ids): entity.handleSyncHidden() for each id
@Remote blockSnapshot(short amount, byte[] data) [UDP]: read pos+blockId, tile.build.readSync(reads, version)
@Remote stateSnapshot(waveTime, wave, enemies, paused, gameOver, timeData, tps, rand0, rand1, coreData):
read team core item data per team

activity_phases.label: Snapshot Timeout Detection
steps: Log.err('Timed out after not received UDP snapshots.')

activity_phases.label: Disconnect & Kick Handling
steps: else show kick dialog unless reason.quiet

conditions_and_branches: net.client() false after reset → disconnectQuietly() [connect canceled]
Data timeout (30s while connecting) → disconnect with '@disconnect.data'
Snapshot timeout (20s in-game, UDP) → disconnect with '@disconnect.snapshottimeout'
kick reason==serverRestarting → reconnect flow instead of error dialog
Packet length <36 bytes OR StreamChunk → skip LZ4 compression
entitySnapshot: entity exists vs new creation → snapSync only if newly created
entitySnapshot: entity not in 'removed' set → add to world
state.isMenu() → skip paused/playing state set in stateSnapshot
packetSpamLimit exceeded on server → DOS blacklist + close connection
playerLimit exceeded on server connect → close connection
key_relationships: ArcNetProvider owns arc.net.Client and arc.net.Server instances
NetClient depends_on ArcNetProvider (via Net facade) for send/disconnect
NetClient uses Timekeeper for rate-limited sync: 66ms/500ms/1000ms intervals
WorldStream delivered via TCP streaming (StreamBegin + StreamChunk[])
key_packets: ConnectPacket (outbound TCP) — name, locale, mods, mobile, versionType, color, usid, uuid
WorldStream (inbound TCP stream) — compressed world data
hiddenSnapshot (inbound UDP) — ids of entities to hide
important_notes: ConnectPacket sent via reliable TCP; clientSnapshot sent via unreliable UDP
World data decompressed with InflaterInputStream before NetworkIO.loadWorld()
Entity deduplication via 'removed' IntSet — prevents re-adding disconnected entities
Snapshot timeout = 20s; data timeout = 30s (1800 game ticks at 60tps)
PacketSerializer: LZ4 compression applied when serialized size >= 36 bytes
finishConnecting() posts Call.connectConfirm via Core.app.post (deferred to next frame)
reconnect flow triggered only for KickReason.serverRestarting
worldDataBegin is a @Remote call — server triggers it before streaming world
